titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Consul General at Shanghai ( Cabot )Repeated to the Ambassador in China as No. 322.

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Consul General at Shanghai ( Cabot )Repeated to the Ambassador in China as No. 322.
div.p:
449. While Dept realizes practical problems to which ConGen doubtless refers (Contel 462 Mar 3 to Emb rptd Dept 735 and Contel 399 Feb 18 to Emb rptd Dept 589Latter not printed.) Dept believes desirable avoid statements which might be open interpretation legal moral obligation impartiality between sides China exists on part this Govt or its natls. US has openly generously aided Natl Govt and has never recognized
belligerency Commie. This position in full accord internatl law and practice.

Dept had understood Chin Govt does not regard present strife China as “civil war”. Emb comment requested whether Sun’sSun Fo, President of the Chinese Executive Yuan (premier). reference civil war (third para Contel 742 Mar 4 Vol. viii, “Political and military situation in China”, chapter II. press summary to Dept rptd unnumbered Emb) indicates change Chin Govt position this question.

If proposed contribution mentioned reftel not legally enforceable tax, ConGen shld so advise Amer natls and shld advise further that they are obligated pay only taxes officially imposed, as provided art 10 1946 treaty and that any case duress shld be reported ConGen.
